tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Cape Verde's Dream: First World Cup Goal and a Mother's Visa
stories.summary: World Cup debutants Cape Verde scored their first-ever goal and held Uruguay to a draw, with their 40-year-old goalkeeper's mother finally able to watch from the stands.

stories.headline: From Homelessness to Hero: Iranian Goalkeeper's Masterclass
stories.summary: Goalkeeper Alireza Beiranvand, who rose from homelessness, delivered seven crucial saves including a stunning save-of-the-tournament contender as Iran held Belgium scoreless at the World Cup.

stories.headline: Community Lifts Up: Strangers Rally to Save Retired Nurse's Home
stories.summary: When Beverly Thomas, a 79-year-old retired nurse facing court over her yard, dozens of volunteers including David Courtwright and special education teacher Melanie Parker showed up to transform her overgrown property.

stories.headline: Against All Odds: Wyndham Clark Wins US Open as His Father Watches
stories.summary: Wyndham Clark won his second U.S. Open at Shinnecock Hills, overcoming a hostile crowd and a shrinking lead to triumph by one stroke with mental coach Julie Elion's support.

stories.headline: Community Says 'I Do': Wedding Celebration for Couple Rising From Homelessness
stories.summary: Abdul and Teresa Broner, once sheltering at a church after losing jobs and housing, celebrated their wedding at that same shelter after securing new jobs driving city buses for Tempe.

stories.headline: Police and Community Build a Home for an Elderly Woman in Need
stories.summary: Police and the Agumbe Lions Club built a permanent home for 70-year-old Gowrajji, who had lived in a makeshift shelter of wooden poles and plastic for decades on Agumbe's fringes.

stories.headline: Extinct Sturgeon Swims Home: Natural Spawning Returns to China's Yangtze
stories.summary: On April 14, Yangtze sturgeon spawned naturally in the wild for the first time since being declared extinct in 2022, marking the species' triumphant return to its original spawning ground.

stories.headline: Stem Cells Offer 15-Year Freedom From Severe Autoimmune Disease
stories.summary: A man and woman with rare autoimmune disease NMOSD have thrived for over 15 years after stem-cell transplants, with the man resuming normal life and becoming a father of two.

stories.headline: A Boy's Tiny Boxes Spread Joy Across Three Oklahoma Towns
stories.summary: Smiley Elmore Jr. creates wooden treasure boxes filled with handwritten notes and gifts, spreading joy across three Oklahoma towns through his quiet, ongoing generosity.

stories.headline: One Woman's Quiet Compassion Rescues 5,000 Forgotten Lives
stories.summary: Vasanthi Manokaran, a 56-year-old Chennai social activist, has rescued and cared for more than 5,000 abandoned elders and forgotten strangers over nearly two decades of unwavering dedication.

stories.headline: Grade 6 Students Help Bring Atlantic Salmon Back to Lake Ontario
stories.summary: Grade 6 students released 93 salmon into Duffins Creek, joining a heartfelt community effort to restore Atlantic salmon to the lake's waters.

stories.headline: Wireless Brain-Computer Interface Restores Communication for Patient in Need
stories.summary: Neurosurgeons at University of Michigan implanted the Connexus wireless brain-computer interface into a Michigan woman with motor neuron disease, restoring her ability to communicate through synthesized speech.

stories.headline: Mi'kmaq Nation Reclaims Nearly 3,400 Acres of Traditional Lands
stories.summary: The Mi'kmaq Nation reclaimed Mesgilg Maqamigew, a nearly 3,400-acre parcel near Bridgewater that will more than double their territory and restore hunting and gathering grounds for future generations.

stories.headline: New DNA Test Slashes Rare Disease Diagnosis Time From Months to Weeks
stories.summary: Researchers at Radboudumc tested 832 patients and found a single revolutionary DNA test can diagnose rare diseases faster than standard methods, cutting diagnostic limbo from months to weeks.
